preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an intent-aware, ai-first design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Slim hero providing immediate context: this is MODE, the intent-aware, AI-first design system for startups. Gives the page a clear title and frames the decision moment that follows, satisfying the mandatory hero requirement for conversion-stage pages.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: The Validator archetype demands proof before committing. Inserting a focused evidence beat—key metrics, adoption stats, or a compact case-study data point—directly after the hero satisfies this psychological need and builds confidence right before pricing.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators also need trust signals. A brief credibility beat with customer logos, testimonials, or notable startup endorsements reinforces the evidence just presented and removes remaining doubt before the decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Conversion-stage funnel requires a Decision beat before Conversion. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparison so the startup can evaluate commitment level with full clarity, framed by the proof they just absorbed.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: The primary CTA and commitment prompt. Placed immediately before Recovery as required. Captures the action—sign-up or checkout—while momentum from evidence, credibility, and decision framing is at its peak.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat providing secondary paths (contact sales, explore docs, FAQ links) and standard footer. Catches any visitor not ready to convert and ensures no dead-end exits.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Slim hero providing immediate context: this is MODE, the intent-aware, AI-first design system for startups. Gives the page a clear title and frames the decision moment that follows, satisfying the mandatory hero requirement for conversion-stage pages.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Conversion-stage funnel requires a Decision beat before Conversion. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparison so the startup can evaluate commitment level with full clarity, framed by the proof they just absorbed.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: The primary CTA and commitment prompt. Placed immediately before Recovery as required. Captures the action—sign-up or checkout—while momentum from evidence, credibility, and decision framing is at its peak.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Conversion stage demands minimal variant: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' This removes distractions and preserves the persistent CTA, 'the most converted element on most SaaS pages.'

Conversion funnel stage rule requires text-only: 'For conversion stage use text-only: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.'

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: The semantic intelligence layer for code-first SaaS teams. Components that resolve behavior, copy density, and visual emphasis to the right experience — without manual variants.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype with conversion intent requires highlighted variant to establish recommendation: 'highlighted variant for recommended plan — use sparingly (one per pricing section). Validator variant includes description and longer feature list.'

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, token config, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever — no runtime dependency, no lock-in.
features: Semantic resolution engine — 4-dimension model (archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode)
Component manifest — 11 components, fully specified with slot schemas and zone affinity
IA planner + multi-variant generation powered by Claude Opus
Three palette presets — funnel-driven, archetype-driven, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map and accent configuration
Lifetime access — lives in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard

page.reasoning: Validator archetype demands trust signals in conversion context: 'Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). minimal for high-frequency use — above footer, between sections.'

slots.headline: The intelligence layer is ready. Is your design system?
slots.subhead: Running in under an hour. Configured once. No Figma handoff cycles, no manual variants, no ongoing subscription.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Own the code. No usage-based billing, ever.

page.reasoning: Conversion stage on pricing page requires recovery mechanism: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
